jokes.text: House run away? Dog's on fire?
jokes.setup: Marge wakes Homer asking for a sign he's awake
jokes.punchline: Homer's hilariously backwards priorities - assumes house mobility and canine combustion before romance issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - escalates from mundane to absurd. Economical but character-specific absurdity

jokes.text: It's 9:30 p.m., and you spent your whole Saturday...drinking beer in Maggie's kiddie pool.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he worked all day and it's 3 AM
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals the pathetic reality - he's drinking in a baby pool on Saturday evening
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff revealing Homer's delusion vs reality. Visual of grown man in kiddie pool is absurd

jokes.text: We shouldn't have put a fireplace in the bedroom.
jokes.setup: Marge wants to deal with romance issues from a book
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point, blames bedroom fire hazard
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur. Misses emotional point for literal interpretation

jokes.text: How many times can you laugh at that cat getting hit by the moon?
jokes.setup: Discussion about TV show quality
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on repetitive cartoon violence while being a cartoon
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about cartoon violence from within a cartoon. Meta but earned

jokes.text: Yes, and when was the last you heard anyone talk about Ren & Stimpy?
jokes.setup: Discussion of clip shows and Ren & Stimpy doing them
jokes.punchline: Sharp observation about how clip shows kill show relevance
jokes.explanation: Brutally accurate media criticism delivered with Lisa's intelligence. Meta-humor that cuts deep

jokes.text: ...by giving in to their throbbing biological urges.
jokes.setup: Marge reading wholesome children's book about bunnies
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly explicit language in kids' book
jokes.explanation: Unexpected adult language in children's context. Somewhat cheap but effective

jokes.text: She's faking it.
jokes.setup: Watching bunny wedding/honeymoon in kids' video
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical adult observation about sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed, unexpectedly adult insight from a child. Maximum impact with minimal words

jokes.text: Romance is dead. It was acquired in a hostile takeover by Hallmark and Disney...homogenized and sold off piece by piece.
jokes.setup: Marge insists romance isn't dead
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated corporate critique of commodified romance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate satire delivered by 8-year-old with adult sophistication. Perfect character integration

jokes.text: Phone call for Al. Al Coholic.
jokes.setup: Prank phone call to Moe's Tavern
jokes.punchline: Classic prank name - 'Alcoholic'
jokes.explanation: Classic but predictable prank call humor. Nostalgic but not original

jokes.text: Homer Sexual! Mike Rotch! Amanda Huggenkiss! Hugh Jass. Ivana Tinkle.
jokes.setup: Continuation of prank calls
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of increasingly obvious dirty pun names
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons prank names. Low-brow but well-executed within character

jokes.text: I'm a stupid moron with an ugly face and a big butt...and my butt smells and I like to kiss my own butt.
jokes.setup: Bart's prank call backfires
jokes.punchline: Bart forced to insult himself in increasingly childish terms
jokes.explanation: Good reversal with escalating self-deprecation. Childish language fits character

jokes.text: Chocolate. Invisible cola. Forbidden donut. 'Sacrilicious.'
jokes.setup: Asked what his heart's fondest desire is
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-obsessed stream of consciousness, ending with made-up word
jokes.explanation: 'Sacrilicious' is perfect Homer - combines sacred/delicious into food-obsessed theology. Peak character writing

jokes.text: No, no. Homer is my ball's name. I'm Marge.
jokes.setup: Jacques calls her Homer after seeing bowling ball
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals the thoughtless gift - Homer bought himself a bowling ball for her birthday
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal of Homer's selfishness. Well-structured misdirection with character truth

jokes.text: Call me Jacques.
jokes.setup: Marge calls him Mr. Brunswick (bowling company)
jokes.punchline: Reveals his pretentious French name - classic bowling alley lothario
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jacques as stereotypical smooth-talking Frenchman. Functional character establishment

jokes.text: Pick up Homer. Pick him up.
jokes.setup: Jacques teaching Marge to bowl
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - picking up the bowling ball named Homer vs picking up Homer himself
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay using the established bowling ball name. Subtle innuendo

jokes.text: My mind says stop, but my heart and my hips cry, 'Proceed!'
jokes.setup: Jacques declaring attraction to married Marge
jokes.punchline: Adding 'hips' to the mind/heart conflict makes it absurdly physical
jokes.explanation: Subverts romantic cliché by adding bodily function. Fits Jacques' character

jokes.text: It's not quite breakfast or lunch...but it comes with a slice of cantaloupe at the end. You don't get what you would at breakfast, but you get a good meal.
jokes.setup: Marge asks 'What's brunch?'
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed, confusing explanation that makes brunch sound terrible
jokes.explanation: Funny deconstruction of brunch concept. Rambling fits character but loses economy points

jokes.text: Thank goodness I drove down that ironic street.
jokes.setup: Marge coincidentally encounters Homer on the street
jokes.punchline: Marge acknowledges the contrived plot convenience
jokes.explanation: Self-aware lampshading of coincidental plot device. Meta humor that works

jokes.text: So if you mentally snip out the part where I already had a husband...that's my idea of romance.
jokes.setup: Marge finishing her romantic story
jokes.punchline: Casually asking kids to ignore the adultery aspect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery of moral compromise. Perfect for Marge's character

jokes.text: Ain't you never seen a naked chick riding a clam before?
jokes.setup: Homer staring at something off-screen
jokes.punchline: Reveals Homer's staring at absurd workplace art, not Mindy
jokes.explanation: Unexpected visual absurdity. Great misdirection from romantic tension

jokes.text: I guess we'll be going down together. I mean, getting off togeth... I mean...
jokes.setup: Homer and Mindy in elevator
jokes.punchline: Homer's accidental sexual double entendres while flustered
jokes.explanation: Classic sexual innuendo setup. Predictable but fits Homer's bumbling character

jokes.text: I'll just push the button for the stimulator... I mean, elevator.
jokes.setup: Continuation of elevator awkwardness
jokes.punchline: More Freudian slips mixing sexual and mechanical terms
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern but less inventive. Relies on obvious sexual wordplay

jokes.text: All I'll use this bed for is sleeping, eating...and maybe building a little fort. That's it!
jokes.setup: Homer resisting temptation in hotel room
jokes.punchline: Innocent childhood activity (fort-building) as resistance to adultery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer childishness as moral defense. Original and character-specific

jokes.text: Let's call room service!
jokes.setup: Mindy suggests a 'wicked idea that could get us into trouble'
jokes.punchline: Subverts sexual expectation with innocent room service
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Builds sexual tension then deflates with mundane reality

jokes.text: Fly, my pretties. Fly!
jokes.setup: Burns sending flying surveillance devices
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference for corporate espionage
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns villainy with pop culture reference. Fits character well

jokes.text: Thanks for poisoning the planet, bastard! Get bent! No more Chernobyls! Go to hell!
jokes.setup: Environmental protesters outside nuclear convention
jokes.punchline: Increasingly aggressive and profane 'peace' protesters
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic contrast between peaceful cause and aggressive methods

jokes.text: The cookie told me so.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he must have sex with Mindy
jokes.punchline: Treats fortune cookie as divine commandment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - avoids moral responsibility by blaming baked goods. Character-specific absurdity

jokes.text: She hit the bottle hard and lost her job.
jokes.setup: Kids ask what happened to Mindy
jokes.punchline: Implies alcoholic downfall rather than revealing choice of Marge
jokes.explanation: Dark humor deflecting from romantic choice. Economical but somewhat cheap resolution

jokes.text: You 'choo-choo-choose' me?
jokes.setup: Ralph reading Lisa's Valentine card
jokes.punchline: Adorably mispronounces 'choose' to fit train theme
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons moment. Perfect child logic and wordplay. Became cultural touchstone

jokes.text: The doctor said I wouldn't have so many nosebleeds if I kept my finger out of it.
jokes.setup: Ralph trying to make conversation with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Overshares gross medical advice as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph-style oversharing. Character-specific awkward humor

jokes.text: Nothing gets chocolate out. See?
jokes.setup: Krusty doing his TV show
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates by spilling more chocolate, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick with Krusty's incompetence. Functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: What does that say? 'Talk to the audience'? Oh, God, this is always death.
jokes.setup: Krusty reading his show notes
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about how audience interaction segments are terrible
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy about the artificial nature of audience participation in TV shows

jokes.text: I love Lisa Simpson! And when I grow up, I'm going to marry her!
jokes.setup: Ralph on live TV with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Public declaration of love on children's show
jokes.explanation: Sets up devastating rejection. Good cringe comedy building to bigger moment

jokes.text: Now, you listen to me! I don't like you! I never liked you! The only reason I gave you that valentine...is because nobody else would!
jokes.setup: Ralph's public declaration of love
jokes.punchline: Lisa's brutal public rejection escalating to devastating revelation
jokes.explanation: Devastating escalation. Each phrase gets crueler. Sets up next iconic moment

jokes.text: You can actually pinpoint the second when his heart rips in half.
jokes.setup: Ralph's devastating public rejection
jokes.punchline: Clinical observation of emotional destruction
jokes.explanation: Legendary Simpsons moment. Perfect dark comedy timing. Became cultural reference for heartbreak

jokes.text: I have a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Girl Bart likes has something important to tell him privately
jokes.punchline: Devastating news delivered as if it's good news
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection. Economy of words for maximum impact. Classic romantic disappointment

jokes.text: There's a whole lot of frowning going on.
jokes.setup: Family discussing failed romance stories
jokes.punchline: Parodies Jerry Lee Lewis song 'Whole Lot of Shakin'' with emotional opposite
jokes.explanation: Decent musical parody fitting the melancholy mood. Not exceptional but works

jokes.text: Well, as Jerry Lee Lewis would say: 'There's a whole lot of frowning going on.'
jokes.setup: Everyone having depressing love stories
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Whole Lotta Shakin' Going On' with frowning
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits the situation but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Soon I will kill you.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions Aunt Selma got married
jokes.punchline: Wedding vow includes death threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob character moment. Dark humor in wedding context. Economical delivery

jokes.text: That's not my mother.
jokes.setup: Abraham sent to pick up his mother for dinner
jokes.punchline: Reveals the woman isn't his mother but he proceeds anyway
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor. Abe's confusion/indifference to wrong person works for character

jokes.text: I really can't.
jokes.setup: Jacqueline says she can't remember when she felt this young
jokes.punchline: Literal inability to remember due to age/dementia
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aging/memory loss delivered matter-of-factly. Economical and effective

jokes.text: I can't remember when I've felt this young. / I really can't.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment during dancing
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation revealing memory loss
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor about aging and memory loss disguised as romantic sentiment

jokes.text: No need for the blown gasket, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Grampa getting agitated about Burns cutting in
jokes.punchline: Burns's condescending automotive metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization with his old-fashioned slang and condescension

jokes.text: I don't wanna be either.
jokes.setup: Asked to choose between marrying Burns or Abe
jokes.punchline: Rejects both suitors equally
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic triangle. Jacqueline's practical wisdom cuts through sentiment

jokes.text: That seems more like a detached tale of modern alienation.
jokes.setup: Marge calls grandfather's story a happy ending
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated literary criticism of the story structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on narrative structure. Perfect for Lisa's intellectual character

jokes.text: Yeah, learned it screws everybody up.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if anyone learned about love
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical conclusion from all the stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart cynicism. Economical summary of episode's dark theme

jokes.text: Soon, Cory, soon.
jokes.setup: Lisa decides to save love for someone guaranteed not to reject her
jokes.punchline: Reveals romantic obsession with teen heartthrob Corey
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intellectual facade hiding normal preteen crushes

jokes.text: I'd appreciate it if you didn't tell anybody about my busy hands.
jokes.setup: Artie after inappropriate advance on Marge
jokes.punchline: Calls unwanted groping 'busy hands'
jokes.explanation: Awkward euphemism for sexual assault. Uncomfortable humor that lands but problematic

jokes.text: All right! All right! I'll walk in the mud!
jokes.setup: Homer giving Marge a ride, presumably arguing about car position
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic capitulation to walk rather than share car space
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish dramatics. Visual gag of grown man walking in mud rather than compromise

jokes.text: All right! All right! I'll walk in the mud!
jokes.setup: Marge making Homer walk on her side of the car
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic capitulation to walking in mud
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing young Homer's dramatic but ultimately accommodating nature

jokes.text: Once you stop this car, I'm gonna hug you and kiss you...and I'll never let you go.
jokes.setup: Young Homer being romantic with Marge
jokes.punchline: Sweet romantic declaration that sounds slightly threatening
jokes.explanation: Homer's romantic intensity borders on possessive. Sweet but slightly ominous undertone
